content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
June 20, 1990


Paul Chrisman
Hyde, Miller & Savage
428 J Street, Suite 400
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re:  Your Request for Confirmation of
     Telephone Advice
     Our File No. I-90-394

Dear Mr. Chrisman:

     We have received your letter dated May 31, 1990 which appears
to seek written confirmation of telephone advice provided to you
by this agency on May 30, 1990, generally regarding the responsi-
bilities of an employee of your client, a county in a redevelop-
ment project, under the conflict of interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act ("the Act").1  The advice that was provided
to you was limited to the provisions of the Act and did not ad-
dress other conflict of interest laws which might be applicable to
your query. Because your request does not appear to involve a
specific pending decision, we are treating it as one for informal
assistance only, pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (copy enclosed).2

     The advice provided to you was premised on the specific facts
you provided in the telephone conversation, to wit: an assistant
planner employed by the county was contemplating a real estate
purchase in a redevelopment area, the employee's duties included
providing assistance to an outside consultant with respect to the
preparation of the redevelopment plan for the county's board of
supervisors, and the functions pertaining to the employee's job
duties did not involve the exercise of discretion and were, in-
stead, "more or less" ministerial.

     On the basis of these facts, you were advised that the em-
ployee did not appear to have a conflict of interest under the

_________________________
1  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2  Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section
83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

File No. I-90-394
Page 2

Act. You were also advised that the duties of the employee would
determine whether he would be required to refrain from participat-
ing in decisions concerning the redevelopment area after the close
of escrow for the real estate purchase. Your letter recognized
that such a job description would be necessary in order for this
question to be addressed. Because no information with respect to
such duties was provided, we were unable to provide you with ad-
vice on this question.

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this mat-
ter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division
